words.definitionEn: to strongly criticize somebody/something that you think is wrong, illegal, etc.
words.exampleEn: denounce somebody/something, She publicly denounced the government's handling of the crisis.

words.definitionEn: feelings of anger and hate that may result in threatening or violent behaviour
words.exampleEn: The research shows that computer games may cause aggression.

words.definitionEn: containing a lot of people, things, plants, etc. with little space between them
words.exampleEn: a dense crowd/forest

words.definitionEn: the quality of being dense; the degree to which something is dense
words.exampleEn: The population density in this city is very high.

words.definitionEn: the state of needing the help and support of somebody/something in order to survive or be successful
words.exampleEn: Our relationship was based on mutual dependence.

words.definitionEn: to show an image of somebody/something in a picture
words.exampleEn: depict somebody/something (as somebody/something), a painting depicting the Virgin and Child

words.definitionEn: to move soldiers or weapons into a position where they are ready for military action
words.exampleEn: 2 000 troops were deployed in the area.

words.definitionEn: the act of moving soldiers or weapons into a position where they are ready for military action
words.exampleEn: the deployment of peacekeeping forces

words.definitionEn: to put money into a bank account
words.exampleEn: Millions were deposited in Swiss bank accounts.

words.definitionEn: a person who is the next most important person below a business manager, a head of a school, a political leader, etc. and who does that person’s job when they are away
words.exampleEn: I'm acting as deputy till the manager returns.

words.definitionEn: to come or go down from a higher to a lower level
words.exampleEn: The plane began to descend.

words.definitionEn: an action of coming or going down
words.exampleEn: The plane began its descent to Heathrow.

words.definitionEn: to say officially that somebody/something has a particular character or name; to describe somebody/something in a particular way
words.exampleEn: be designated (as) something, This area has been designated (as) a National Park.

words.definitionEn: that you would like to have or do; worth having or doing
words.exampleEn: She chatted for a few minutes about the qualities she considered desirable in a secretary.

words.definitionEn: a screen on a computer that shows the icons of programs and files that can be used
words.exampleEn: desktop icons

words.definitionEn: causing destruction or damage
words.exampleEn: The war demonstrated the destructive power of modern weapons.

words.definitionEn: to keep somebody in an official place, such as a police station, a prison or a hospital, and prevent them from leaving
words.exampleEn: One man has been detained for questioning.

words.definitionEn: connected with the science or practice of farming
words.exampleEn: agricultural policy/land/production/development

words.definitionEn: the process of discovering or noticing something, especially something that is not easy to see, hear, etc.; the fact of being discovered or noticed
words.exampleEn: crime prevention and detection

words.definitionEn: the state of being kept in a place, especially a prison, and prevented from leaving
words.exampleEn: They were sentenced to 12 months' detention in a young offender institution.

words.definitionEn: to become worse
words.exampleEn: Her health deteriorated rapidly, and she died shortly afterwards.

words.definitionEn: to completely destroy a place or an area
words.exampleEn: The bomb devastated much of the old part of the city.

words.definitionEn: the most powerful evil being
words.exampleEn: belief in the Devil

words.definitionEn: to invent something new or a new way of doing something
words.exampleEn: A new system has been devised to control traffic in the city.

words.definitionEn: to say exactly what an illness or the cause of a problem is
words.exampleEn: diagnose something, The test is used to diagnose a variety of diseases.

words.definitionEn: the act of discovering or identifying the exact cause of an illness or a problem
words.exampleEn: a diagnosis of lung cancer

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody what to do, especially in an annoying way
words.exampleEn: dictate something (to somebody), They are in no position to dictate terms (= tell other people what to do).

words.definitionEn: a political leader who has complete power over a country, especially one who has gained it using military force
words.exampleEn: The country suffered at the hands of a series of military dictators.

words.definitionEn: to recognize or show that two things are not the same
words.exampleEn: differentiate (between) A and B, It's difficult to differentiate between the two varieties.

words.definitionEn: a calm and serious manner that deserves respect
words.exampleEn: She accepted the criticism with quiet dignity.

words.definitionEn: a situation that makes problems, often one in which you have to make a very difficult choice between things of equal importance
words.exampleEn: I could see no way of resolving this moral dilemma.

words.definitionEn: a measurement in space, for example how high, wide or long something is
words.exampleEn: We measured the dimensions of the kitchen.

words.definitionEn: to become smaller, weaker, etc.; to make something become smaller, weaker, etc.
words.exampleEn: The world's resources are rapidly diminishing.

words.definitionEn: to put something quickly into a liquid and take it out again
words.exampleEn: dip something (into something), He dipped the brush into the paint.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to represent his or her country in a foreign country, for example, in an embassy
words.exampleEn: Washington's top diplomat in Havana

words.definitionEn: connected with managing relations between countries (= diplomacy)
words.exampleEn: a diplomatic crisis

words.definitionEn: a book or electronic resource containing lists of information, usually in alphabetical order, for example people’s phone numbers or the names and addresses of businesses in a particular area
words.exampleEn: a telephone/trade directory

words.definitionEn: a person who helps another person, especially a politician, in their job
words.exampleEn: White House aides

words.definitionEn: very bad, harmful or unsuccessful
words.exampleEn: a disastrous harvest/fire/result

words.definitionEn: to get rid of something that you no longer want or need
words.exampleEn: discard somebody/something, The room was littered with discarded newspapers.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody official permission to leave the police or the armed forces; to make somebody leave the police or the armed forces
words.exampleEn: be discharged from something, He was discharged from the army following his injury.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody information about something, especially something that was previously secret
words.exampleEn: disclose something (to somebody), The spokesman refused to disclose details of the takeover to the press.

words.definitionEn: the act of making something known or public that was previously secret or private
words.exampleEn: the newspaper’s disclosure of defence secrets

words.definitionEn: a long and serious treatment or discussion of a subject in speech or writing
words.exampleEn: discourse on something, a discourse on issues of gender and sexuality

words.definitionEn: the freedom or power to decide what should be done in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: I'll leave it up to you to use your discretion.

words.definitionEn: the practice of treating somebody or a particular group in society less fairly than others
words.exampleEn: age/racial/gender/sex discrimination (= because of somebody’s age, race or sex)

words.definitionEn: the act of dismissing somebody from their job; an example of this
words.exampleEn: He still hopes to win his claim against unfair dismissal.

words.definitionEn: to take the place of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: be displaced (by somebody/something), Gradually factory workers have been displaced by machines.

words.definitionEn: the act of getting rid of something
words.exampleEn: The council is responsible for waste disposal and street cleaning.

words.definitionEn: to arrange things or people in a particular way or position
words.exampleEn: The visitors disposed themselves in a circle round the statue.

words.definitionEn: an argument between two people, groups or countries; discussion about a subject on which people disagree
words.exampleEn: industrial/pay disputes

words.definitionEn: to question whether something is true or legally or officially acceptable
words.exampleEn: dispute something, These figures have been disputed.

words.definitionEn: to make it difficult for something to continue in the normal way
words.exampleEn: Demonstrators succeeded in disrupting the meeting.

words.definitionEn: a situation in which it is difficult for something to continue in the normal way; the act of stopping something from continuing in the normal way
words.exampleEn: We aim to help you move house with minimum disruption to yourself.

words.definitionEn: to mix with a liquid and become part of it
words.exampleEn: Salt dissolves in water.

words.definitionEn: a clear difference or contrast especially between people or things that are similar or related
words.exampleEn: distinctions between traditional and modern societies

words.definitionEn: having a quality or characteristic that makes something different and easily noticed
words.exampleEn: clothes with a distinctive style

words.definitionEn: to change the shape, appearance or sound of something so that it is strange or not clear
words.exampleEn: a fairground mirror that distorts your shape

words.definitionEn: a feeling of great worry or unhappiness; great mental pain
words.exampleEn: The newspaper article caused the actor considerable distress.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody feel very worried or unhappy
words.exampleEn: distress somebody, It was clear that the letter had deeply distressed her.

words.definitionEn: making you feel anxious and upset or shocked
words.exampleEn: a disturbing piece of news

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/something change direction
words.exampleEn: be diverted, The course of the stream has now been diverted.

words.definitionEn: coming from or connected with God or a god
words.exampleEn: divine law/love/will

words.definitionEn: a belief or set of beliefs held and taught by a Church, a political party, etc.
words.exampleEn: the doctrine of parliamentary sovereignty

words.definitionEn: the documents that are required for something, or that give evidence or proof of something
words.exampleEn: I couldn't enter the country because I didn't have all the necessary documentation.

words.definitionEn: an area of knowledge or activity; especially one that somebody is responsible for
words.exampleEn: Financial matters are her domain.

words.definitionEn: the fact of being more important, powerful or easy to notice than somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: political/economic dominance

words.definitionEn: a person or an organization that makes a gift of money, clothes, food, etc. to a charity, etc.
words.exampleEn: international aid donors (= countries that give money, etc. to help other countries)

words.definitionEn: an amount of a medicine or a drug that is taken once, or regularly over a period of time
words.exampleEn: a high/low/lethal dose

words.definitionEn: although
words.exampleEn: He finally agreed, albeit reluctantly, to help us.

words.definitionEn: to make something empty or dry by removing the liquid from it; to become empty or dry in this way
words.exampleEn: Drain and rinse the pasta.

words.definitionEn: to move along smoothly and slowly in water or air
words.exampleEn: Clouds drifted across the sky.

words.definitionEn: strong and powerful; having a strong influence in making something happen
words.exampleEn: Who was the driving force (= the person with the strongest influence) in the band?

words.definitionEn: to die because you have been underwater too long and you cannot breathe; to kill somebody by holding them underwater
words.exampleEn: Two children drowned after falling into the river.

words.definitionEn: having two parts or aspects
words.exampleEn: his dual role as composer and conductor

words.definitionEn: to give somebody/something a particular name, often in a humorous or critical way
words.exampleEn: The media dubbed anorexia ‘the slimming disease’.

words.definitionEn: able to think quickly; quick to notice things
words.exampleEn: Suddenly he found himself awake and fully alert.

words.definitionEn: stupid
words.exampleEn: That was a pretty dumb thing to do.

words.definitionEn: two people who perform together or are often seen or thought of together
words.exampleEn: the comedy duo Laurel and Hardy

words.definitionEn: the way in which people or things behave and react to each other in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: the dynamics of political change

words.definitionEn: a situation in which people are watching for danger and ready to deal with it
words.exampleEn: on the alert (for something), Police are warning the public to be on the alert for suspicious packages.

words.definitionEn: very interested and excited by something that is going to happen or about something that you want to do; showing this
words.exampleEn: eager crowds outside the stadium

words.definitionEn: the money that you earn for the work that you do
words.exampleEn: a rise in average earnings

words.definitionEn: to warn somebody about a dangerous situation or one that requires immediate action
words.exampleEn: alert somebody, Neighbours quickly alerted the emergency services.

words.definitionEn: lack of difficulty
words.exampleEn: with ease, He passed the exam with ease.

words.definitionEn: to become less unpleasant, painful or severe; to make something less unpleasant, etc.
words.exampleEn: The pain immediately eased.

words.definitionEn: strange and frightening; different from what you are used to
words.exampleEn: an alien environment

words.definitionEn: the reflecting of sound off a wall or inside a particular space so that a noise appears to be repeated; a sound that is reflected back in this way
words.exampleEn: There was an echo on the phone and I couldn't hear clearly.

words.definitionEn: if a sound echoes, it is reflected off a wall, the side of a mountain, etc. so that you can hear it again
words.exampleEn: Her footsteps echoed in the empty room.

words.definitionEn: connected with the relation of plants and living creatures to each other and to their environment
words.exampleEn: We risk upsetting the ecological balance of the area.

words.definitionEn: extremely silly; not logical and sensible
words.exampleEn: That uniform makes the guards look absurd.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to teach or educate people
words.exampleEn: adult educators (= who teach adults)

words.definitionEn: to arrange something in the correct position, or to be in the correct position, in relation to something else, especially in a straight line
words.exampleEn: Make sure the shelf is aligned with the top of the cupboard.

words.definitionEn: the fact of producing the result that is wanted or intended; the fact of producing a successful result
words.exampleEn: to check the effectiveness of the security system

words.definitionEn: the quality of doing something well with no waste of time or money
words.exampleEn: improvements in efficiency at the factory

words.definitionEn: your sense of your own value and importance
words.exampleEn: He has the biggest ego of anyone I've ever met.

words.definitionEn: arrangement in a straight line
words.exampleEn: the alignment of the sun, moon and earth at a particular time

words.definitionEn: very complicated and detailed; carefully prepared and organized
words.exampleEn: elaborate designs

words.definitionEn: connected with elections
words.exampleEn: electoral systems/reforms

words.definitionEn: very similar
words.exampleEn: My sister and I do not look alike.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody/something a higher position or rank, often more important than they deserve
words.exampleEn: elevate somebody/something (to something), He elevated many of his friends to powerful positions within the government.

words.definitionEn: in a very similar way
words.exampleEn: They tried to treat all their children alike.

words.definitionEn: a person who is eligible for something or to do something, is able to have or do it because they have the right qualifications, are the right age, etc.
words.exampleEn: eligible (for something), Only those over 70 are eligible for the special payment.

words.definitionEn: a small group of people in a society, etc. who are powerful and have a lot of influence, because they are rich, intelligent, etc.
words.exampleEn: a member of the ruling/intellectual elite

words.definitionEn: to get onto a ship or plane; to put somebody/something onto a ship or plane
words.exampleEn: We stood on the pier and watched as they embarked.

words.definitionEn: shy, uncomfortable or guilty feelings; a feeling of being embarrassed
words.exampleEn: I nearly died of embarrassment when he said that.

words.definitionEn: a group of officials led by an ambassador who represent their government in a foreign country
words.exampleEn: embassy officials

words.definitionEn: to fix something in a substance or solid object
words.exampleEn: be embedded in something, an operation to remove glass that was embedded in his leg

words.definitionEn: to express or represent an idea or a quality
words.exampleEn: embody something, a politician who embodied the hopes of black youth

words.definitionEn: the fact of somebody/something moving out of or away from something and becoming possible to see
words.exampleEn: the island’s emergence from the sea 3 000 years ago

words.definitionEn: based on experiments or experience rather than ideas or theories
words.exampleEn: empirical evidence/knowledge/research

words.definitionEn: to give somebody the power or authority to do something
words.exampleEn: be empowered (to do something), The courts were empowered to impose the death sentence for certain crimes.

words.definitionEn: to pass a law
words.exampleEn: (be) enacted (by somebody/something), legislation enacted by parliament

words.definitionEn: to include a large number or range of things
words.exampleEn: The job encompasses a wide range of responsibilities.

words.definitionEn: the act of encouraging somebody to do something; something that encourages somebody
words.exampleEn: a few words of encouragement

words.definitionEn: that gives somebody support, courage or hope
words.exampleEn: This month's unemployment figures are not very encouraging.

words.definitionEn: an attempt to do something, especially something new or difficult
words.exampleEn: There have been great advances in the field of scientific endeavour.

words.definitionEn: very large in size or amount and seeming to have no end
words.exampleEn: endless patience

words.definitionEn: to say publicly that you support a person, statement or course of action
words.exampleEn: I wholeheartedly endorse his remarks.

words.definitionEn: a public statement or action showing that you support somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The election victory is a clear endorsement of their policies.

words.definitionEn: to experience and deal with something that is painful or unpleasant without giving up
words.exampleEn: endure something, They had to endure a long wait before the case came to trial.

words.definitionEn: to make sure that people obey a particular law or rule
words.exampleEn: enforce something, It's the job of the police to enforce the law.

words.definitionEn: the act of making people obey a particular law or rule
words.exampleEn: strict enforcement of regulations

words.definitionEn: an agreement to marry somebody; the period during which two people are engaged
words.exampleEn: Their engagement was announced in the local paper.

words.definitionEn: interesting or pleasant in a way that attracts your attention
words.exampleEn: an engaging smile

words.definitionEn: a large quantity that is more than enough
words.exampleEn: The brochure promised beautiful walks with an abundance of wildlife.

words.definitionEn: to ask somebody for some information
words.exampleEn: enquire about somebody/something, I called the station to enquire about train times.

words.definitionEn: to improve the quality of something, often by adding something to it
words.exampleEn: The study of science has enriched all our lives.

words.definitionEn: to arrange for yourself or for somebody else to officially join a course, school, etc.
words.exampleEn: You need to enrol before the end of August.

words.definitionEn: to happen after or as a result of another event
words.exampleEn: An argument ensued.

words.definitionEn: a company or business
words.exampleEn: He is in charge of an enterprise with a turnover of $26 billion.

words.definitionEn: a person who is very interested in something and spends a lot of time doing it
words.exampleEn: a football enthusiast

words.definitionEn: a public statement that is made without giving proof, accusing somebody of doing something that is wrong or illegal
words.exampleEn: to investigate/deny/withdraw an allegation

words.definitionEn: to give somebody the right to have or to do something
words.exampleEn: be entitled to something, You will be entitled to your pension when you reach 65.
